note: Editorial broadcast-intro copy for the Atlas — one to two evocative sentences per nation, written to be distinct per team. This is presentation flavour, not a sourced data field. Powers the typewriter reveal in the country panel.

intros.Argentina: Argentina, where football is scripture and Diego is a saint, arrives as the reigning order of the world — albiceleste, unhurried, and certain.
intros.Spain: Spain, the land that taught the ball to speak, still passes its way through defences like water finding the sea.
intros.France: France, a republic of talent renewed with every generation, brings a squad deep enough to field two contenders and win with either.
intros.England: England, the country that wrote the laws of the game, carries a century of longing and a golden generation finally in its prime.
intros.Portugal: Portugal, perched on the Atlantic's edge, blends old seafaring daring with a conveyor belt of ruthless, modern attacking talent.
intros.Brazil: Brazil, the land where the beautiful game was named, chases a sixth star with the swagger only five titles can buy.
intros.Morocco: Morocco, gateway between Africa and Europe, arrives no longer as a surprise but as the semi-finalists who rewrote the continent's ceiling.
intros.Netherlands: The Netherlands, birthplace of Total Football, plays the game as geometry — space invented, space exploited, orange in full flow.
intros.Belgium: Belgium, a small country of high ambition, returns to prove its golden generation still has one great tournament left in it.
intros.Germany: Germany, engineered for tournaments and forged in finals, believes as an article of faith that the machine always finds its rhythm.
intros.Croatia: Croatia, a nation of four million with the heart of forty, keeps turning up in the last rounds long after the odds say it should not.
intros.Colombia: Colombia, where the game moves to a cumbia beat, plays with joy in its feet and steel in its spine.
intros.Mexico: Mexico, a co-host roaring on home soil, carries the green of a country that treats the World Cup as a national holiday.
intros.Senegal: Senegal, the Lions of Teranga, prowl out of West Africa with power, pace, and a champion's recent pedigree.
intros.Uruguay: Uruguay, the smallest nation ever to rule the world, still plays with la garra charrúa — the claw that refuses to let go.
intros.United States: The United States, hosts on their own turf, arrive with a European-schooled generation ready to turn curiosity into belief.
intros.Japan: Japan, disciplined and fearless in equal measure, keeps closing the gap on the giants one precise, patient tournament at a time.
intros.Switzerland: Switzerland, quietly formidable and impossible to hurry, is the tournament team that always makes you work for every inch.
intros.Iran: Iran, Team Melli of the Persian plateau, defends like a fortress and asks every opponent to find a way through.
intros.Turkey: Turkey, straddling two continents, returns with a young, fearless generation and the noise of a nation behind it.
intros.Ecuador: Ecuador, drawn from the thin Andean air, runs and presses with a youthful fearlessness that wears bigger names down.
intros.Austria: Austria, schooled in the relentless press, arrives as the well-drilled outsider nobody wants to draw.
intros.South Korea: South Korea, tireless and quick, plays every match at a sprint and carries the flag of a whole continent's expectation.
intros.Australia: Australia, the Socceroos from football's furthest frontier, answer every question with running, grit, and refusal.
intros.Algeria: Algeria, the Desert Foxes of the Maghreb, mix North African flair with a fierce competitive edge.
intros.Egypt: Egypt, the most decorated name in African football, arrives chasing the world stage its continental history demands.
intros.Canada: Canada, the land of maple and a co-host awakening, brings the fastest, boldest team in its footballing history to its own party.
intros.Norway: Norway, land of fjords and long winters, finally brings a generation of genuine stars back to the game's biggest stage.
intros.Ivory Coast: Ivory Coast, the Elephants of West Africa, march in with power up front and the memory of continental glory still fresh.
intros.Panama: Panama, the bridge between two Americas, plays with the pride of a small nation that fought for every yard to be here.
intros.Sweden: Sweden, cool and organised from the Nordic north, trusts structure and set-pieces to trouble anyone.
intros.Czechia: Czechia, heirs to a proud central-European lineage, combine technical craft with a stubborn tournament resilience.
intros.Paraguay: Paraguay, hard-running and harder to break, plays the uncompromising, combative football of the South American heartland.
intros.Scotland: Scotland, the Tartan Army made flesh, brings raw passion and a nation that will out-sing any stadium it enters.
intros.Tunisia: Tunisia, the Eagles of Carthage, defend with discipline and carry the hopes of the Mediterranean's southern shore.
intros.DR Congo: DR Congo, the Leopards of central Africa, arrive brimming with athletic power and a hunger long denied a global stage.
intros.Uzbekistan: Uzbekistan, first-timers off the old Silk Road, step onto the world stage to announce Central Asia has arrived.
intros.Qatar: Qatar, recent hosts turned qualifiers, bring the patient, possession-minded football of a project built to last.
intros.Iraq: Iraq, the Lions of Mesopotamia, carry the resilience of a football nation that has known triumph against every odd.
intros.South Africa: South Africa, Bafana Bafana of the rainbow nation, return to the world stage with the rhythm and roar of home behind them.
intros.Saudi Arabia: Saudi Arabia, the Green Falcons, arrive with the memory of felling giants and the ambition to do it again.
intros.Jordan: Jordan, debutants from the crossroads of the Levant, write the first World Cup chapter in their nation's history.
intros.Bosnia and Herzegovina: Bosnia and Herzegovina, the Dragons of the Balkans, play with the fire and flair of a proud, resilient young nation.
intros.Cape Verde: Cape Verde, the Blue Sharks of a ten-island archipelago, are the tiny nation whose fairytale qualification stunned a continent.
intros.Ghana: Ghana, the Black Stars of West Africa, return with the swagger of a country that has come agonisingly close before.
intros.Curaçao: Curaçao, a sun-bleached speck in the Caribbean, become the smallest nation ever to reach a World Cup — and they belong.
intros.Haiti: Haiti, first Caribbean pioneers back after half a century, carry the defiant joy of a people who never stopped dreaming.
intros.New Zealand: New Zealand, the All Whites from the edge of the map, cross oceans as Oceania's lone standard-bearer to test themselves against the world.
